title: Beehiiv vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and Buttondown email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which suits your newsletter needs.
intro: Beehiiv and Buttondown are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Beehiiv focuses on newsletter monetization and growth-oriented publishers, while Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers who value markdown simplicity. This comparison will help you determine which platform is right for your newsletter.
choose_a_if: You want to monetize your newsletter through ads or paid subscriptions
You need to manage large subscriber lists (2,500+ on free tier)
You're looking for advanced growth features like referral programs and segmentation
choose_b_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown format
You need API access and technical flexibility
You want the most affordable paid plan starting at just $9/month

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. Beehiiv excels at monetization and scaling with larger audiences, while Buttondown is better for technical users who prefer simplicity and markdown. Beehiiv has higher ratings on both G2 (4.7) and Trustpilot (4.4), though Buttondown ranks slightly higher on G2 (4.8) and significantly higher on Trustpilot (4.7).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown offers a more affordable paid plan starting at $9/month compared to Beehiiv's $39/month. However, Beehiiv's free tier accommodates 2,500 subscribers versus Buttondown's 100, making Beehiiv better value if you have a growing audience. Your choice depends on subscriber count and budget.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import and export functionality, making migration possible. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a key feature, and Beehiiv's analytics and segmentation tools can help you rebuild your audience structure. Plan ahead for any automation or workflow changes during the transition.
